| [IPPC Facility Emission Points](https://www.isde.ie/geonetwork/srv/api/records/e7d08424-f256-46ca-bafe-c37d64ae74a8?language=all/formatters/xml) | failed validating <Element '{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}verticalCRS' at 0x7f7394786de0> with XsdGroup(model='sequence', occurs=[0, 1]):Reason: Unexpected child with tag '{http://www.opengis.net/gml/3.2}VerticalCRS' at position 1.Schema component:  <xs:complexType xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" name="SC_CRS_PropertyType">      <xs:sequence minOccurs="0">          <xs:element ref="gml:AbstractCRS" />      </xs:sequence>      <xs:attributeGroup ref="gco:ObjectReference" />      <xs:attribute ref="gco:nilReason" />  </xs:complexType>Instance type: <class 'xml.etree.ElementTree.Element'>Instance:  <ns0:verticalCRS xmlns:ns0="http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd" xmlns:ns1="http://www.opengis.net/gml/3.2" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">    <ns1:VerticalCRS ns1:id="vertical-crs-01">      <ns1:description>An Ordnance Datum or OD is a vertical datum used by an ordnance survey as the basis for deriving altitudes on maps. A spot height may be expressed as AOD for "above ordnance datum". Usually mean sea level (MSL) is used for the datum.</ns1:description>      <ns1:descriptionReference />      <ns1:identifier codeSpace="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ordnance_Datum">Malin Ordnance Datum</ns1:identifier>      <ns1:domainOfValidity />      <ns1:scope>Derivation of vertical height or depth (in metres)</ns1:scope>      <ns1:verticalCS />      <ns1:verticalDatum>        <ns1:VerticalDatum xsi:type="gml:VerticalDatumType" ns1:id="vertical-crs-02">          <ns1:description>In the Republic of Ireland, OD for the Ordnance Survey of Ireland is Malin Ordnance Datum: the Mean Sea Level (MSL) at Portmoor Pier, Malin Head, County Donegal, between 1960 and 1969.</ns1:description>          <ns1:descriptionReference />          <ns1:identifier codeSpace="http://www.opengis.net/def/crs/EPSG/0/5731">5731</ns1:identifier>          <ns1:name>EPSG Projection 5731 - Malin Head height</ns1:name>          <ns1:remarks>Metres</ns1:remarks>          <ns1:scope>Derivation of Altitude</ns1:scope>          <ns1:anchorDefinition>OD based on Mean Sea Level, Malin Head 1960-1969</ns1:anchorDefinition>        </ns1:VerticalDatum>      </ns1:verticalDatum>    </ns1:VerticalCRS>  </ns0:verticalCRS>Path: /{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}MD_Metadata/{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}identificationInfo/{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}MD_DataIdentification/{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}extent/{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}EX_Extent/{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}verticalElement/{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}EX_VerticalExtent/{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}verticalCRS |

| [IPPC Facility Monitoring Points](https://www.isde.ie/geonetwork/srv/api/records/08fb1e98-0cae-4a5f-8051-bcd9d245194a?language=all/formatters/xml) | failed validating <Element '{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}verticalCRS' at 0x7f7394726480> with XsdGroup(model='sequence', occurs=[0, 1]):Reason: Unexpected child with tag '{http://www.opengis.net/gml/3.2}VerticalCRS' at position 1.Schema component:  <xs:complexType xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" name="SC_CRS_PropertyType">      <xs:sequence minOccurs="0">          <xs:element ref="gml:AbstractCRS" />      </xs:sequence>      <xs:attributeGroup ref="gco:ObjectReference" />      <xs:attribute ref="gco:nilReason" />  </xs:complexType>Instance type: <class 'xml.etree.ElementTree.Element'>Instance:  <ns0:verticalCRS xmlns:ns0="http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd" xmlns:ns1="http://www.opengis.net/gml/3.2" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">    <ns1:VerticalCRS ns1:id="vertical-crs-01">      <ns1:description>An Ordnance Datum or OD is a vertical datum used by an ordnance survey as the basis for deriving altitudes on maps. A spot height may be expressed as AOD for "above ordnance datum". Usually mean sea level (MSL) is used for the datum.</ns1:description>      <ns1:descriptionReference />      <ns1:identifier codeSpace="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ordnance_Datum">Malin Ordnance Datum</ns1:identifier>      <ns1:domainOfValidity />      <ns1:scope>Derivation of vertical height or depth (in metres)</ns1:scope>      <ns1:verticalCS />      <ns1:verticalDatum>        <ns1:VerticalDatum xsi:type="gml:VerticalDatumType" ns1:id="vertical-crs-02">          <ns1:description>In the Republic of Ireland, OD for the Ordnance Survey of Ireland is Malin Ordnance Datum: the Mean Sea Level (MSL) at Portmoor Pier, Malin Head, County Donegal, between 1960 and 1969.</ns1:description>          <ns1:descriptionReference />          <ns1:identifier codeSpace="http://www.opengis.net/def/crs/EPSG/0/5731">5731</ns1:identifier>          <ns1:name>EPSG Projection 5731 - Malin Head height</ns1:name>          <ns1:remarks>Metres</ns1:remarks>          <ns1:scope>Derivation of Altitude</ns1:scope>          <ns1:anchorDefinition>OD based on Mean Sea Level, Malin Head 1960-1969</ns1:anchorDefinition>        </ns1:VerticalDatum>      </ns1:verticalDatum>    </ns1:VerticalCRS>  </ns0:verticalCRS>Path: /{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}MD_Metadata/{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}identificationInfo/{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}MD_DataIdentification/{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}extent/{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}EX_Extent/{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}verticalElement/{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}EX_VerticalExtent/{http://www.isotc211.org/2005/gmd}verticalCRS |
